titleStmt.title: SII 27.230: original edition by M. D. Sampath, H. K. Narasimhamurthy, Madhav N. Katti and B. R. Gopal
No. 230. (A.R. No. 550 of 1929-30.) PAḌUVARI, COONDAPUR TALUK, SOUTH KANARA DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab set up in a field.
The record is dated Śaka 12[4]6, Raktākshi, Siṁha 17, Wednesday corresponding to 1324 A.D., August 14, the weekday being, however, Tuesday.

It records a gift of land, situated in Bayidūru, excluding the dēvasva belonging to god Baṁkēśvara to god Sōmanātha and another deity (name lost), by the king and Siṁgaṇṇa-sāhaṇi, grandson of mahāpradhāna Sōvaṇṇa-sēnabōva• It states that Mallikārjuna-gōpa, son of • • dādēvi was to protect the gift.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
namastuṁga śiraścuṁbi caṁdracāmara cāravē trailōkya nagarāraṁbha
mūlastaṁbhāya śaṁbhavē . svastiśrīmatu sakavaruṣa 1246
neya raktākṣi saṁvatsarada siṁhamāsa 17 neya budhavāraṁdaṁ
du svasti śrī matpāṁḍya cakravartti Arirāya basava saṁkhara śrī vīra
sōidēvāḷpēṁdradēvarasaru śrīmanu mahāpradhānaṁ sōva
ṁṇa sēnabōvaramaṁ maga siṁgaṁṇa sāhaṇi baradu koṭṭa
bayidūra nāḍa Adhikāri lōkiyaḍahara muṁtāgi ba
Idūra haravari yoḷake toḷahara baḷiya dātunāya kana
da bāḷu mūḍalu soḷeyamakaḷa bāḷa basurigoḷagaḍi paḍuva
lu samudragaḍi teṁkalu yisvara sēnabōvana vōṇigaḍi baḍagalu
māvina kuḷigaḍi yiṁtī nākugaḍiyiṁdoḷagaṇa bāḷinoḷage ba
ṁ kēśvaradēvara dēvasva bhāratamaki hoṟagāgi vuḷida bāḷanū
Ā bāḷiṁ saluva makimane ki niṭṭilu koṁtāri mu
ṁtāda bāḷanu Ā bāḷiṁge baṁda teṟuvoḷage
yellavanu koyidu kaṟukeyakirttaḍe nātha sōma
nātha dēvariṁge Ā caṁdrārkkasthāyiyāgi dhārāpū
rvvakavāgi yeṟadu koṭṭaru yi dēvagaḷa bhāryye
dādēviya kumāra mallikārjuna gōpagaḷu
yi dharmma pratipālakaru bayidūra nāḍava koḍa
varu vakrastharādaḍe vāraṇāśiyali sāvirakavileya ko
ṁdavaru keṭiya narakadalikkidavaru . svadattāṁ pa
radattaṁ vā yō harēta vasuṁdharā śaṣṭivarṣa sahasrā
ṇi viṣṭāyāṁ jāyatē krimi .
radāta
Digital edition of SII 27.230 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
